source: Weyns & Calinescu 2015 [1] Table III (7-service TAS catalogue); topology and variable ranges from __OLD__/data/config/cs1/default_qn_model.csv and __OLD__/data/config/cs1/default_dim_variables.csv.
notes: 13-artifact queueing network, baseline (no adaptation). TAS_1..TAS_6 are composite workflow stages; MAS_1..MAS_3 are Medical Analysis Services; AS_1..AS_3 are Alarm Services; DS_3 is the Drug Service. External arrivals enter at TAS_1 with lambda_z=345 req/s.
TODO_revisit_rates: REVISIT mu (service rates) and lambda_z (arrival rate) before locking the comparison. The Camara/Weyns numbers (mu in [150, 1580] req/s, lambda_z=345) come from a Java/ReSeP stack; the FastAPI prototype caps at ~200 req/s sustained per `src.methods.calibration --rate-sweep --rate-sweep-target-loss 1.0`. With the seeded values the prototype is over-driven (~7 % effective-rate loss at lambda_z=345), so analytic vs experiment comparisons in 07-comparison.ipynb will be biased by client undershoot rather than measuring the actual DASA tech-agnostic claim. Two options: (a) scale lambda_z down to the prototype-sustainable rate while keeping mu ratios; (b) bump mu uniformly so the prototype headroom matches Camara's. Decide before running 06-yoly-experimental.ipynb at scale.

c_{TAS_{1}}.description: Number of resources allocated in TAS_1

K_{TAS_{1}}.description: Allocated request capacity in TAS_1 memory buffer

M_{act_{TAS_{1}}}.description: Active memory used by processing requests in TAS_{1} (M_act = L · delta).

M_{buf_{TAS_{1}}}.description: Allocated buffer memory for the TAS_{1} queue (M_buf = K · delta).

L_{TAS_{1}}.description: Average number of request in TAS_1 component

L_{q, TAS_{1}}.description: Average number of request in TAS_1 memory

W_{TAS_{1}}.description: Average time to complete a request in TAS_1

W_{q, TAS_{1}}.description: Average time for a request to transit the buffer in TAS_1

d_{TAS_{1}}.description: Data density for request in TAS_1 expected data per requests

_labels.baseline: Baseline: no adaptation. MAPE-K inert. TAS workflow and service catalogue as published in Weyns & Calinescu 2015 [1] Table III.

environments._note: `_setpoint` names the default scenario for this config — the loader reads this scenario when no `--adaptation` CLI flag is given. `_routs[scenario][i][j]` = routing probability from node i to node j under `scenario`. Position i indexes into `_nodes[scenario]`. Diagonal captures per-service error probability; off-diagonal captures the TAS workflow routing.
